title: fix: accept chat responses without model
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Allows non-streaming chat completion responses without a `model` field.
- Falls back to the resolved request model only for usage headers when upstream omits `model`.
- Adds community endpoint regression coverage.
- Tests: `npx biome check --write shared/schemas/openai.ts gen.pollinations.ai/src/community-endpoints.test.ts gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/handler.ts`; `npm run test -- src/community-endpoints.test.ts --testNamePattern "routes chat completions through a registered community endpoint with its saved token"`; `npm run typecheck`.
- Fixes #12142

gist.headline: No model? No problem.
gist.blurb: We've made our chat API a little more forgiving. If an upstream provider forgets to include their model name in the response, we'll just politely fill it in for them instead of throwing an error.
gist.summary: The text generation API now accepts non-streaming chat completion responses that omit the `model` field, falling back to the requested model name for usage headers.
gist.impact: Developers using community endpoints or upstream providers that don't return a model field will no longer encounter validation errors.
gist.visual_concept: The bee (Polly) and Nomnom creature. Nomnom is eating an API response that has a missing piece (an empty box). Polly is happily handing Nomnom the missing piece to complete the data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot handing a missing 'model' puzzle piece to the round tan Nomnom creature, who is happily eating a stack of API response documents. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
